archive.name: main

item.title: among the dormant watchers to be released in december 2018 on inverse records

archive.item: After almost ten years in production, the third full-length "Among the Dormant Watchers" is now scheduled for release in December 2018 on Inverse Records.

item.title: six waves of woe nominated: best doom metal album 2008

archive.item: "six waves of woe" has been nominated as best doom metal album 2008 at www.metalstorm.ee with the motivation: Four years after the majestic "Departure", Sweden's best kept Doom-secret Forest Of Shadows returns with another tear-jerking, emotionally draining masterpiece. "Six Waves Of Woe" continues the base elements present on "Departure", but is more than just that. More mechanical and industrial than the debut and also more to the point, the album emits a devestation glow of isolation and coldness. Can you handle this much heartless music?

I am currently preparing some re-releases of "where dreams turn to dust" and "departure" that will be released thru firebox. Both albums will include some old remastered songs as bonus tracks. More info will be presented here within the coming weeks.

"Six Waves of Woe" is now a closed chapter. It was mastered by Minerva Pappi at Finnvox, Finland some week ago and the result has been sent to Firebox together with the case-layouts. All that remains now is the actuall release which is scheduled for the 29th of October this year. If you haven't already noticed, two songs from "six waves of woe" are available for previewing, one at myspace and one at firebox's webpage. Check them out if you haven't.

After more than three years of composing, recording and mixing I now consider "six waves of woe" finished or at least ready for mastering. All that's left now before I leave it in the hands of the label is to finish up the case-layout. The release-date is currently being discussed and I think we can expect the album to be out this autumn/winter. Stay tuned for mp3-samples that will be posted here and on myspace and for more details about the release-date.

Finally, all the vocals have been recorded and I'm currently arranging it all to something nice. At the same time I'm finishing the final polishing and adding the last pieces to bring the songs together. I will soon start pre-mastering the songs while waiting for the details about the release, that when and how it all will happen. I'll let you all know as soon as it gets settled and also stay tuned for thoose teaser samples.

Just felt that I needed to let you all know that the album is still progressing. I have recorded most vocals but I guess there is still some work to do before I am satisified. One or two songs are near completion though so I might put up a teaser sample quite soon so stay tuned for that.

Having completed all the lyrics for the upcoming album I also gave it a name, or at least a working-title - "six waves of woe". More information about this can be found in the discography section where I will write about its progress from this day on until the release.

So, I finally got some copies of the katatonia tribute both on DLP and DCD. Nice. If you are a fan of katatonia and especially their earlier works I would really recommend you to check it out (http://www.northern-silence.de/). I'm still working on the previously mentioned new stuff and it's progressing slow but well. I hope to be able to give you all some more information about this soon.

I just saw some updates about the katatonia tribute and the final call is that it will be out on the 12th of December. It will be released both on DCD and DLP and have a total playtime of over 100 minutes. While I'm writing I might as well confirm that the band is still alive despite lack of updates. I'm working on some new material and have been doing so for quite some time now but I don't want to give away any offical details about anything since I might not live up to it.

Northern Silence seems to have had some problems with arranging the katatonia tribute but now things are coming together and the planned release-date is set to beginning of 2006. Forest of Shadows will appear on the album with "rainroom", which also can be found on the '99 promo.
The release-date for the re-release of "where dreams turn to dust" has been set to the 23rd of November.

A remastered digipak version of the mCD "where dreams turn to dust" will be released by Firedoom. The actuall release-date hasn't been decided yet but I'll post it here on the website as soon as I know.

I'm preparing a re-release of the mCD "where dreams turn to dust" since it has run out of stock at most distros. Also the company that used to be responsible for it (Rage of Achilles) has gone out of business and thus won't be able to make a second press. I'll get back with more information like release-date and which label that will take care of the release.
Departure has been voted as the #1 album of 2004 at "Lost Stats Domain" (http://www.zenial.nl). At the same site Forest of Shadows is currently voted 2nd best metal band of all time :)
The katatonia cover "rainroom", originally found on the '99 promo, will be featured on a katatonia tribute which is to be released during early 2005 on northern silence.

archive.name: site
archive.item: This site is now hosted by github and may be getting some renovations further on

item.title: among the dormant watchers added to discography

archive.item: "Among the Dormant Watchers" has been added to discography with lyrics and information.
Two new interviews have been added.
New visuals to match the new album: "Among the Dormant Watchers".
Added some more reviews of "where dreams turn to dust".
Added the entire demo "the silent cry" from 1998 in mp3-format.
Added the russian press of "departure".
Added some more reviews of "six waves of woe".
I added some reviews of "six waves of woe" and some fresh interviews.
The biography section has now been updated and exposed. This means that the entire page is now up.
The discography section has now been updated and exposed. I hope to be able to finish the rest within the next week or so.

As you might have noticed the webpage has been given a major facelift to match the new album. You may also notice that the current page doesn't offer as much as it used to and that's because it's simply not finished. Still I decided to put it up in it's current state to present at least news and contact information. The rest will be put online any day during the coming weeks.
Added the album-cover for "six waves of woe" and some information about the album.
Added the latest progress of "six waves of woe".
Information about the upcoming album "six waves of woe" has been added to the discography section. From this day on until the release this page will preset the progress of the album.
Added the compilation "december songs (a tribute to katatonia)" to the discography section.
Added a review of "where dreams turn to dust" from the metal observer
Added a review of "where dreams turn to dust" from imhotep
Added the remaster of "where dreams turn to dust" to the discography section.
Added some reviews.
Added some interviews.
Yet another interview has been added.
Another interview has been added.
Two interviews and one review has been added.
Added links to the distributors of "departure", "conquest for the empire" and "where dreams turn to dust".
Loads of interviews from 2000-2005 has been added.
Alot of old reviews has been added. Coming up shortly will be loads of interviews and articles.
Reviews of "departure" from terrorizer and close-up has been added as well as some old reviews of "where dreams turn to dust".
More reviews of "departure" has been added. Old reviews of "where dreams turn to dust" and the promos are coming up shortly as well as interviews and articles.

The press section is now open and it and at this stage it includes some of the many reviews of "departure". More reviews of this album as well the older albums will be added in the coming days. I will also try and add some interviews and articles quite soon.
Alot of downloadables such as mp3's and case-layouts has been added. More will be added once I get some more space.
I have added some pictures from the 1999 photo-session.
I have added some pictures from the 2002 photo-session.
It is now possible to view the member profiles and some of the band pictures. The rest of the pictures will be up within a week or so.
The biography section has now been enabled but is restricted to the history sub-section. Information about members as well as the photo gallery will be up shortly.
The new site is finally up. It's not 100% finished though but I will try and have all the features up within a month or so.

archive.name: related
archive.item: I have left shubend since I don't have any time or energy to invest in that band. Dan and Martin will continue making music together though but under the name "genesis of pain".
